actual.topTitle: KESAVANANDA BHARATI SRIPADA GALVARU;RAGHUNATH RAO GANPAT RAO;SHETHIA MINING AND MANUFACTURING CORPORATION LIMITED;ORIENTAL GOAL COMPANY LIMITED V/S STATE OF KERALA;UNION OF INDIA
actual.answerSummary: AIR 1973 SC 1461 corresponds to Kesavananda Bharati Sripadagalvaru & Ors. v. State of Kerala & Ors., decided by the Supreme Court of India on 24 April 1973. The recorded proceedings in the supplied evidence show this petition challenged the constitutionality of the Twenty-fourth, Twenty-fifth and Twenty-ninth Constitutional Amendment Acts and raised questions about the interpretation of Article 368, with factual background from Kerala land reform legislation and asserted violations of fundamental rights (Articles 14, 19, 25, 26, 31). The provided evidence is partial and does not include the Court’s full reasoning or final operative holding; it only outlines the issues and the structure of the judgment. If you need the full text of the judgment or its authoritative holdings (for example on amendment power or the “basic structure” doctrine), I can retrieve or summarize those from the complete judgment text.

actual.topTitle: RAVAL SHAILESHBHAI RAMESHBHAI VIRCHANDBHAI V/S STATE OF GUJARAT
actual.answerSummary: The latest murder-related Gujarat authority visible in the supplied evidence is Raval Shaileshbhai Rameshbhai Virchandbhai v. State of Gujarat, decided by the High Court of Gujarat (D.B.) on 17 February 2026. From the excerpt, it concerns a fatal knife assault that began with a quarrel and led to a prosecution under Section 302 IPC after the injured person died during treatment. The next newest visible decision is Pintubhai @ Kalio Dolubhai Vasava v. State of Gujarat, decided on 12 February 2026, also by the Gujarat High Court (D.B.), involving a conviction under Sections 302/114 IPC arising from the killing of Parvatiben in Bharuch district. An older recent Gujarat High Court (D.B.) decision in the evidence is Raju @ Rajubhai Haribhai Gohel v. State of Gujarat, decided on 09 December 2025, concerning a Rajkot murder prosecution involving knife and pipe assault. So, on the material provided, 17 February 2026 is the newest visible decision date.

actual.topTitle: RAVAL SHAILESHBHAI RAMESHBHAI VIRCHANDBHAI V/S STATE OF GUJARAT
actual.answerSummary: In **Raval Shaileshbhai Rameshbhai Virchandbhai v. State of Gujarat, 2026 LawSuit(Guj) 327**, the prosecution case, from the extract provided, is that what began as casual interaction turned into a quarrel after the accused allegedly used highly abusive language toward the deceased. When the deceased objected and did not retaliate, the accused is said to have suddenly pulled out a folding knife and inflicted repeated blows on the stomach, chest, abdomen, hand, shoulder, and neck, including blows to vital parts. The injuries were described as fatal and life-threatening, and an eyewitness who tried to intervene was also injured. The legal significance of the visible extract is that it focuses on **intention and the nature of the assault**: repeated knife blows aimed at vital parts are relied on to suggest an intention to cause death, which is central in a murder prosecution. The practical takeaway is that in stabbing cases arising from a quarrel, courts closely examine the weapon used, number and placement of injuries, and whether the attack appeared sudden but deliberate. The final ruling or disposition is not fully visible in the evidence provided.
